summary.one_line: The session materially improved the workspace operating system by clarifying contracts, eval memory, calibration boundaries, and operator-agent doctrine.

artifacts_reviewed: git history for recent system commits

issues.title: Promotion path from eval to doctrine is still implicit
issues.description: The session surfaced the need for a standard describing when a lesson graduates from local calibration into cross-skill principle.
issues.operator_takeaway: The next philosophical step is to make promotion criteria explicit.

issues.title: Operator handoff exists strongly in video but is not yet generalized
issues.description: The pattern is real, but it remains domain-local in implementation.
issues.operator_takeaway: Watch whether handoff becomes a cross-workflow primitive before canonizing it further.

issues.title: Ask / infer / default is powerful but not yet fully elevated
issues.description: The rubric emerged clearly in discussion but has not yet been promoted into the top-level runtime constitution as a named rule.
issues.operator_takeaway: Pressure-test it in real runs before moving it upward.

recommendations.action: Add a future section on how lessons are promoted from eval to skill to cross-skill doctrine.
Watch for repeated non-video handoff patterns before standardizing a general handoff artifact or protocol.
Consider promoting ask / infer / default once it proves stable across more workflows.

feedback_hooks.question: Which ideas from OPERATING_PRINCIPLES.md continue to feel true after several more real runs?
feedback_hooks.how_to_compare: Revisit future system-level evals and see which principles recur naturally in decisions and edits.

feedback_hooks.question: Does operator handoff become a recurring pattern outside video?
feedback_hooks.how_to_compare: Track future workflows for explicit human-tool boundaries that need resumable packaging.

core_phrases: The operator owns intent. The agent owns execution.
Human intervention is part of the system, not a failure of the system.
Truth should live where the best evidence exists.
Contracts matter more than local cleverness.
Structure the counts. Narrate the meaning.
The system should encode the kind of certainty it has.
